title: pediomelum subacaule
intro: Pediomelum subacaule, also known as small-flowered lupine or smallflower lupine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it useful for treating conditions such as kidney stones and arthritis. Pediomelum subacaule has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be dried and used to make teas or infusions, or its extracts can be used in capsules or tinctures.
